item.title: Re: Great tool aganst spies
item.content:encoded: I know you wrote this comment over six years ago, but dang that is a great idea that the Foundation should've thought of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've already given suggestions in my original comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm open to suggestions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Plot Twist: The SCP is actually an eldritch god with an endless hunger that can only be sated with sticks and twigs. They throw in some toys just in case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Sticks and branches every few days" plus that single tack-on line at the end of the procedures is still completely inadequate. The cell itself needs a comprehensive edit because it's not appropriate for the SCP that has to live in it.

You decided to make an SCP about a parrot. That means you have signed up for researching appropriate parrot husbandry and applying it to your alcoholic reverse-furry under a punishment curse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Avian enrichment toys added. It already receives sticks and branches every few days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why in the world is there no mention of appropriate enrichment for the enclosure? Anything that's part-parrot (and worse, part-human) would lose its mind in the enclosure described, not to mention likely develop foot sores from only having a layer of newspaper on concrete to stand on.

This creature needs an appropriate substrate for its feet, it needs at least faux vegetation, it needs toys and novel stimuli to occupy its mind with. These things need to be at least touched on in the containment procedures, or else they're not going to happen in-universe. Not providing these things for the SCP is textbook animal cruelty, and the Foundation isn't cruel, just cold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading this, I was imagining nothing less than a creepily realistic Big Bird (although really closer to Abelardo Montoya), and I can't get that out of my head, even knowing it's Jibboo derived. The nest and the counting just made it worse! Knowing I was wrong just makes me feel dirtier for having imagined (with horror) a drunken nymphomaniacal female Maori Big Bird.
To me, it doesn't feel fully supportive of the leak regarding the SCiP, but that doesn't hurt the SCiP, what with "death of the author and all." Taken by itself and setting aside the self-inflicted torture of my childhood - not bad, but didn't impress enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you rub your index and middle finger down the chin, and yeah there might have been some difference but its a moot point from across the living room with my glasses off :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Candy/sweet/sugar/cute

Isn't there a difference in facial expression and movement, even though palm orientation/handshape/location are the same? Technically it'd be two different signs, since I vaguely recall nouns being two movements.

you don't have to listen to me tho all I know is 3 years of ASL from high school

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if the note at the end is accurate, ASL is very similar to maori

For example my wife recently said to me "your candy is in your jacket"

but due to the fact that directly translated to english the asl she said was

you Candy/sweet/sugar/cute in jacket/coat/vest

my brain interpreted it as "you look cute in your jacket"

item.title: Re: Staff Post @AliceinSCPland
item.content:encoded: Usually a staff post is marked "Open" or "Closed" to indicate whether conversation can/may/should continue. It's kind of grey area here, but I think in this case your reply is alright. No worries.

item.title: Re: Staff Post @AliceinSCPland
item.content:encoded:
Um…okay. I thought I did a pretty decent job of reviewing it, but I'll try to be more substantive in the future. Sorry.

Edit: And I just learned from reading other comments that people who aren't staff aren't supposed to respond to staff posts. Again, sorry. >.<

item.title: Staff Post @AliceinSCPland
item.content:encoded: Hey buddy. You're getting awfully close to just spouting erroneous nonsense in place of critique. Your review doesn't have much substance and betrays a thoroughly lackadaisical reading. Please think things over carefully before reviewing. Thanks.

item.title: Re: Hmmm.... (Site Crit Rebuttal)
item.content:encoded:
Given that this is at +63, I'm not sure if what you've suggested wouldn't come off as extraneous information.

Also

a SCP that's only interested in booze and sex really isn't an interesting creature.

This… is not exactly how I'd describe the SCP, personally. I feel like the focus of the article isn't so much on the behavior or the thing itself, but rather what might have happened to whatever it was originally that would make it what it is now.

Stats and behaviors and data data data tend to not hold as much weight if they don't have accompanying implied backstory or parts of an overarching narrative a reader puts together over the course of the piece.

item.title: Re: Hmmm.... (Site Crit Rebuttal)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah but…a SCP that's only interested in booze and sex really isn't an interesting creature. Maybe it doesn't need a complete overhaul, but the character of the bird itself is too dull. Even a real-life parrot has more personality than this. Does it try to escape? Does it try to play with objects? Does it show stress from a lack of social interaction by pulling out its feathers as real life parrots do?

Has the foundation run any kind of experiment to see what its like while drunk? Or any experiments whatsoever? Like giving it a mirror to see if it recognizes the reflection as itself?

There's a lot of potential here that is not being tapped.

item.title: Re: Hmmm.... (Site Crit Rebuttal)
item.content:encoded:
Lots of animals will eat fermented food to get high. I'm not aware of any that will stockpile it with the intent of fermenting it.

The note is ungrammatical because it was written by someone with very poor language skills — perhaps by someone who was in the process of becoming a parrot/emu/human mixture? Or by someone who had difficulty with human languages in the first place? Someone who knows the vocabulary, but doesn't understand the syntax or grammar? (Such as, for instance, someone who found an English/Cook Island Maori glossary somewhere online, and copied out words?)

item.title: Re: Hmmm.... (Site Crit Rebuttal)
item.content:encoded:
On the contrary, I think that there's just enough of this puzzle to be interesting without giving to much away.

  1. Reading the note seems to indicate that it's been cursed for some transgression. And the fact that the note is non-grammatical doesn't necessarily mean Maori isn't its native language; it could be that the entity was once a Maori villager before being cursed, and wrote the note either as its language was fading from comprehension or as an illiterate person might try to write it.
  2. Mating with humans fulfills a biological imperative it doesn't quite understand and can no longer fulfill.
  3. Stockpiling fruit for fermentation so it can eat it and become intoxicated is normal for intelligent non-humans to do; lots of mammals and birds have been observed doing this.

In short, while the article is written at a distance that makes empathy a little tougher, there's nothing wrong with that choice on the face of it. The back-story of this entity is not included in greater detail because the Foundation would have no way of knowing it. And whether or not there are some things that could be made stronger, this article is far from needing a complete overhaul.

You're of course entitled to your opinion, but temperance is the better part of virtue. Every jot and tittle of a story need not be written on the page in order for it to be effectively communicated. Keep this in mind when reading articles in the future.

item.title: Hmmm....
item.content:encoded:
This has promise, but it doesn't go anywhere. Why is the Maori so ungrammatical? Why write a note in the language (which is fairly obscure) if it clearly isn't your native tongue?

And the SCP itself…it doesn't go anywhere either. The other bird SCP (SCP-1192) writes "i wan my mom" and "wy am i a bird" in pencil, which creates sympathy for the plight of the character. But this parrot thing…there's nothing here that anyone can relate to. You can't feel any sympathy or horror or any emotional investment whatsoever for this SCP as it is written. What is its story? What does it want other than alcohol and to mate with humans? What does it feel?

Interesting premise, but very poorly executed, and in dire need of a complete rewrite. You should scrap this version of the parrot-humanoid and start from scratch.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To repeat:

At this point in time, no tests of SCP-1849's reproductive capabilities are authorized.

Do we honestly have to say "DON'T TRY TO HAVE SEX WITH THE SCP" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weirdly interesting. And I'm half-curious of what would happen if someone agreed to try mating with it. Is it like a black widow, just killing its mate afterwards? Are the eggs horny harpy eggs, spawning little horny harpies? Hell, are there even enough genetic similarities for breeding?

item.title: Great tool aganst spies
item.content:encoded: Aside from the sad/tragic implications of this SCP's implied origin/childhood, I can't stop seeing this as a insanely useful tool. Anytime you have a entity you need to interrogate who is non-cooperative, you simply put them in a soundproofed room next to SCP-1849 with a TV that flashes your questions at them by remote. As long as no other intelligences are within broadcast distance, you get a nice clean response in the audio from SCP-1849. While anyone can consciously refuse to verbalize a response to a question, the mind's natural response is to have a solution/reaction to any and all stimuli, and this is an involuntary action. Therefore a perfect non-invasive interrogation technique, also great to passively test personnel's loyalty to the Foundation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Suddenly I realize how stupid that comment was. After so long I finally realized that it said FEATURES and not feathers. I'm such an idiot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…No one said anything about this? Seriously?

more reminiscent of a human’s features than a bird’s

So I guess I'm the only human without feathers then?

Honestly, that line caught me up, and because of it I skimmed the whole article, barely registering any of it.
No vote because I didn't read it (because I couldn't read it)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohhhh! Ok, THAT makes sense.
Sorry, details like -that- mark the difference between learning enough english to understand the stuff I read, and knowing details by living in the country. XD In spanish, wouldn't work that way~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What you've missed is that "newsprint" is not "pages from newspapers". It's "paper of a quality used for newspapers".

You can buy blank newsprint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, can someone tell me how's this using newsprint, instead of something neutral, designed by the foundation? Is there a specific reason why it has to be written material that they don't know if the creature -can- read, and could give it information it shouldn't have?

I assume I missed something, for now… but I think that a place like the foundation wouldn't use newsprint for that unless specifically needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this as having originally been a human that somehow got turned into this bird thing. It marks days on its cell wall and tries to ferment fruit into alcohol. It may even have written that note itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
similar in morphology to both a parrot and emu,

…I'm sorry, my brain is unable to imagine this. I read the rest of the physical description, but it all failed to register because I'm stuck on this bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've seen what they can do with those beaks, right?

Honestly, "don't try to have sex with the SCP" should be understood already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what I would do if I met SCP-1849.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sweet jumpin' jambalaya, Voct you posted up that fix *while I was reading it*. I was like "What is he talking about, in the history that's the exact same note I just read! *closes thing, goes back* HOLY crap okay now it makes sense."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, makes you funnier than me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still interperet it the same way. You know, the standard, self-justifying "look, it was a very attractive chicken!".

…does that make me worse?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My actual inspiration for this thing was the Jibboo, a horribly twisted, dark bird thing from page 17 of Dr. Seuss's "Oh The Thinks You Can Think!" But I like this interpretation much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great Scott, Voct!
Well done, thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll take a guess and say that Voct kept it open to interpretation. As for mine, I'm thinking along the lines that the fey in New Zealand took carnal advantage of some poor Maori girl and this is what came out. But that's my digusting mess of a mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i read it as a note of abject, profoundly embarrassed apology from whatever poor bastard made the thing.

it's very reminiscent of the origin of the chicken lady, which i like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clearly improved, but what the hell is the new note driving at? Is there some Maori mythology involved or is it just cryptic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, the note was what earned it my downvote and now the note (and the rest of the rework) is what earns my upvote. Good job, Voct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well, nobody wants a bird with anthrax on its leg, now do they?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic adpation of the note - you've done it again, Voct!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've completely changed the content of the note tied to the critter's leg. There's still a backstory there, but it may now be somewhat… different.

(When you give me permission to tinker, sometimes I tinker a lot.)

I've also clarified and improved a few details about its anatomy and behavior. "Deep emotional bonding" is pretty subjective; "sexual imprinting" is the accepted term, and gets the idea across more quickly.

The part about visible tattoos is because visible tattoos are common amongst the Māori. This critter clearly prefers Māori to any other humans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct, you wanted me to look at this one, right? I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staying neutral until someone explains that last note thing, went straight over my head.

Also: http://2.bp.blogspot.com/-iarq5sjWDWc/TWRxt8nPegI/AAAAAAAAA_0/ABabl2TlOO0/s1600/emu.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject’s neck, body, wings, and legs are elongated and more reminiscent of a human’s features than a bird’s

its wings look just like human wings

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The note at the end won it my downvote. It was shrugworthy up till then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this should not be a thing, ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I wouldn't put it like that…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "What would you do if a lusty, psychic, mentally handicapped, humanoid parrot-thing wanted your johnson?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would explain the enscription on the band. Actually, I originally misread it as meaning that, which is the main reason I adore this so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thoroughly mediocre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gamma ray? Rhapsody?

Well the metal band bit is there not only to add a bit of backstory, but to see who catches its small reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i read this SCP due to this post; i'm terribly disappointed by the metal band. i was hoping for something more like gamma ray, or maybe rhapsody.

(the metal band bit detracts from the rest, as currently written)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what I was thinking actually. Though I don't see how it could be AWCY. The note seems really misleading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're correct; fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm almost 100% certain that that is someone's fetish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Patricia the psychic, lusty, bird girl who's mentally retarded and shits everywhere".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote leaving it standing at zero as I like birds and don't think we have enouph bird-based SCPs. My only gripe is the metal band, what's the point of it anyway, and the provacitave relationship bit as Lycan Therapy put it.
